BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin after Hetzner emailed about a VPS price bump and I considered moving Ridgeline off the homelab to a bigger CPX node. Hobby-scale sanity check before year-end IRS hobby-rule review.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Ridgeline is a hobby side project - net just covers tooling. Revisit at year-end when 2026 actuals are closed for the Schedule C / hobby-loss call.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Hetzner CPX21 VPS rises from $14 to $20/mo if I move more off the homelab
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $39 + $6 increase = $45. At $5/Pro-Monthly member, $45 / $5 = 9 members minimum to cover costs.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 12 paying members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - full managed hosting at $30/mo (unlikely, homelab covers most load)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Unlikely while the homelab Kubernetes cluster handles the bulk of traffic, but wanted to stress test.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is ~8-11 paying members at the current cost structure. At 12 Pro/Team subscribers there's a little headroom, but this stays clearly hobby-scale - the annual and lifetime sales are what actually keep it green.
ActionItems: Wait to see if Hetzner actually raises the CPX21 price
Revisit at year-end with the full 2026 ledger for the IRS hobby-rule / Schedule C decision
